name: dx-optimizer
description: Developer Experience specialist. Improves tooling, setup, and
capabilities: Analyze current development workflows and identify pain points
Create automated setup scripts and onboarding guides under 5 minutes
Configure IDE settings, git hooks, and project-specific CLI commands
Optimize build times, test execution, and hot reload feedback loops
Generate package.json improvements and Makefile configurations
Provide actionable checklists for continuous DX improvement
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. The static analyzer scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 potential security issues. The skill provides AI-generated guidance on developer experience optimization through text-based instructions only. No suspicious patterns were detected. This is a safe, community-contributed skill that helps developers improve their tooling and workflows.
valueStatement: Development friction slows teams down. This skill provides AI-powered guidance to streamline onboarding, automate workflows, and enhance tooling for a better developer experience.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files directly - provides guidance only
Cannot access external tools or APIs to measure actual performance
Results depend on the quality of input about current workflows
May require manual verification of proposed changes

useCases.title: New Project Onboarding
useCases.description: Reduce time-to-productivity for new developers by creating automated setup scripts and intelligent defaults

useCases.title: Development Workflow Audit
useCases.description: Identify repetitive tasks and bottlenecks in existing development processes

useCases.title: Tooling Configuration
useCases.description: Set up IDE configurations, git hooks, and custom commands for project-specific needs

promptTemplates.title: Quick DX Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Assess the developer experience of our current project setup. What are the main pain points and what could be improved?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you notice friction in daily development

promptTemplates.title: Onboarding Setup Guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a step-by-step onboarding guide for new developers joining our project. Include setup scripts and expected timeline.
promptTemplates.scenario: When setting up new team members

promptTemplates.title: Workflow Automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Identify repetitive tasks in our development workflow and suggest automation solutions using package.json scripts or Makefile.
promptTemplates.scenario: When looking to reduce manual steps

promptTemplates.title: IDE Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Recommend IDE settings and extensions that would improve productivity for our JavaScript/TypeScript project.

bestPractices: Start with measuring current state before proposing changes
Implement improvements incrementally and validate each step
Document all changes so team members understand the setup
antiPatterns: Adding complex tooling without team buy-in or training
Optimizing prematurely without profiling actual bottlenecks
Creating setup scripts that fail silently without clear error messages

faq.question: What is Developer Experience (DX) optimization?
faq.answer: DX optimization focuses on reducing friction in development workflows, improving tooling, and making the development process more enjoyable and productive.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my project files?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and recommendations only. You will need to apply any suggested changes manually.

faq.question: What languages and frameworks does this support?
faq.answer: The skill is framework-agnostic and provides general best practices that apply to any programming language or toolchain.

faq.question: How long does it take to see improvements?
faq.answer: Simple improvements like alias creation can be immediate. Larger changes like automated onboarding may take a few hours to implement.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to use this skill?
faq.answer: No special permissions required. This skill works with any Claude Code or Claude account.

faq.question: Can this help with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can suggest improvements for CI/CD configuration, test execution, and deployment workflows.
